rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE CARLOS AND MARTA P. RAMIREZ 1996 REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: continued to 1/24/22. the petition must be amended, as only one accounting can be filed at a time, and for only one trust (not two sub-trusts). the single account may be for a period of no more than two years, but must be presented as an integrated two year accounting (not two - one year accountings). in addition, it is not clear on what basis this court could accept the opening values, as no probate referee has prepared an I & A, no bank statements were filed for the opening date, and no other appraisals were filed setting the value of the real properties.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF VILMA SILVA-RE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Cash was used to pay for caregiver services. Conservators allege that one of the caregivers, who is one of the few caregivers that Conservatee will allow to care for her, insisted on being paid cash. 2) Margaret Silva-Re was paid for cleaning fees, in addition to her allowed stipend. Separate cleaning fees were not charged during the last accounting, and her stipend was reduced in the order approving the 3rd account. 3) Car insurance and a parking permit for an automobile in the joint names of Conservatee and Margaret Silva-Re were paid. Court to determine whether Margaret should be made to contribute for these costs. 4) Bond calculation does not include receipts from trust. Petitioners allege that they are transfers made pursuant to the terms of the trust and are not part of the Conservatee's estate. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HEATHER MALONE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The conservator and attorneys request compensation in the total amount of $8,736.25 for their services rendered during this account period. The conservator took $2,800 as fees on account during this account period. As of 11/03/2021, the estate cash balance is $797. The conservator's Medi-Cal set-aside account balance is approximately $2,000, after payment of the $800 assessment fee to the Court. The conservator's attorneys' fees in the amount of $748.32 allowed on the prior accounting petition remain unpaid. No installment payment plan and other conditions of the payment, if the requested fees are to be allowed in full, have been proposed by the conservator. 2) The Public Guardian does not appear to object to the Court's withdrawal of the authority to take fees on account, but requests blanket authority to transfer funds to the Public Guardian's set-aside account. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HERBERT ALBERT SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues were raised by the previous tentative ruling for the 12/13/2021 hearing and remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) The proof of service shows notice of this petition was given less than 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petitioner has not been reinstated since his authority as administrator of this estate was suspended by the Court. Although the petitioner has not been removed and no vacancy in the office of a personal representative has occurred to appoint a successor personal representative under Probate Code section 8522, the petitioner does not appear to have authority to make the final distribution or perform other acts proposed by the petition. 4) Paragraph 4 of the petition must provide additional information required by CRC 7.403(2) & (3). The amount claimed by Witherspoon & Siracusa appears incorrect. A DE-174 form must also be filed in response to each claim on file. 5) Paragraph 11 must provide an additional allegation as to whether all real property taxes have been paid or are due. 6) Paragraph 11d (incorrectly numbered) alleges that the estate income was deposited in interest-bearing accounts, but paragraph 9 alleges there is no rental income to report. 7) No Schedule C referred to in paragraph 18 was attached. A legal description of the real property of the estate must be provided in the petition and proposed order. 8) The prayer for relief does not address how the petitioner intends to satisfy the Department of Health Care Services' claim. The other creditors' consent to the proposed liens with 7% simple interest starting to accrue from the date of the Court's order for final distribution must also be filed. The consent must clarify whether each creditor is forgoing 5% interest already accrued. 9) All IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a), if taken by the petitioner, must be described. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN LAWERENCE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/31/2022. 3rd continuance for same issues. Matter would go on for hearing for failure to respond to examiner notes but the court calendar is full so the matter has been continued. 1) Assignments filed after the petition for final distribution was filed. It appears the petition needs to be amended to change the distribution and to address LRSF 14.36 B. If distribution is to be made to an assignee of an heir or devisee, the assignee must file the original assignment, and the terms thereof must be disclosed in the petition for distribution. Probate Code 11604. [LRSF 14.36 B.] 2) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 3) If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share, or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. [See LRSF 14.36 G. and Probate Code 11801 et seq.] 4) Please explain and file receipts in support of entry 10/12/2020 Reimbursement for Pre-Sale House Expenses of $10,703.89. 5) The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 01/18/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORNA S. VILLANUEVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Settle First And Final Account And Report Of Former Special Administrator Of The Estate, For Discharge Of Bond, And For Payment Of Extraordinary Fees To Former Special Administrator
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/10/2022 (no earlier date available). The following issues e-mailed to counsel on 12/13/2021 remain unaddressed: 1) A statement required by CRC 7.702(4) & (7) must be filed in support of the petitioner's compensation for extraordinary services. 2) Some of the services described in the petitioner's billing statement appear to be "ordinary services", compensation for which is not allowed until the close of administration, unless the general personal representative joins in the petition for allowance (or the court in its discretion so allows). Probate Code section 8547(b). The compensation for ordinary services must be apportioned among the petitioner, the deceased executor, and the current successor executor. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA G. VELLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/31/2022. Court calendar full. 1)Please file a declaration verified by petitioner that address the following: a. The anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate so the court can determine the amount of bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000.00. [See LRSF 14.16 F.] b. Please explain who is the trustee of the Maria G. Vella Trust and their address. Note they may need to receive notice on DE-121. c. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. See [LRSF 14.18 C. 3)] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MATTER CAMACLANG FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: continued to 1/10/22 for counsel to address proper venue. PC 17005 says the proper county in which to commence an action is county where the principal place of administration is located. the petition states the trustee lives in Solano County and the principal place of administration is in Santa Clara County, within the same district as San Francisco. Venue is not determined by district but by county. San Francisco does not appear to be the proper venue for this petition.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEOFFREY R. BURBIDGE AND ELEANOR MARGARET BURBIDGE FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 10, 2009
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For (1) Order Compelling Trustee To Provide Distributions From The Suport Trust; (2) Breach Of Trust And Removal Of Trustee And Appointment
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although the petition is not procedurally ready to be heard. 1) No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] 2) Not all beneficiaries of the trust are listed in the petition or received notice. Adelaide Hewitt is a beneficiary of a pecuniary bequest. 3) The petition indicates that Geoffrey Burbidge died on 1/26/10, but it does not address whether or not Margaret Burbidge is still living. 4) Un-endorsed filed copies of the proof of service, Nomination of Successor Trustee, and Acceptance of Nomination and Consent to Act as Successor Trustee were delivered to this Examiner. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] It appears that the latter two papers were e-filed on 12/2/21. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.
